title: ev_fire
description: An electric vehicle suffers a lithium-ion battery thermal runaway, creating a prolonged and dangerous fire requiring specialist tactics.

timeline.title: Thermal Event Begins
timeline.description: Driver reports battery warning lights and smoke from the underside of the vehicle.

timeline.title: Fire Ignites
timeline.description: Battery pack ignites. Intense white smoke and flames visible from underside. Crew alerted.

timeline.title: Initial Suppression Attempt
timeline.description: First crew on scene attempts to suppress. Fire resists standard attack — battery re-ignites.

timeline.title: Extended Water Application
timeline.description: High-volume water cooling applied to battery pack. Thermal runaway continuing in cells.

timeline.title: Submersion or Pit Method Considered
timeline.description: Battery fire persisting. Specialist EV salvage container or continued flood cooling discussed.

timeline.title: Fire Controlled
timeline.description: Battery temperature drops below ignition threshold after sustained cooling. Monitoring continues.

timeline.title: Reignition Warning
timeline.description: Vehicle cannot be released to owner — reignition risk within 24-48 hours. Tow to safe holding area.

node-1.title: EV Thermal Runaway
node-1.situation: An electric vehicle is on fire on a residential street. The battery pack is involved — white smoke and intense heat are visible from underneath. The driver has self-evacuated. A standard vehicle fire attack approach is being considered.
node-1.question: How do you approach suppression of an EV battery fire?

options.label: A
options.text: Do NOT use dry powder on the battery. Apply large volumes of water (typically 3,000–10,000 litres) directly to the battery pack from a safe angle, establishing a wide perimeter and treating toxic smoke as a HAZMAT hazard.

options.label: B
options.text: Apply dry chemical extinguisher directly to the flames — this is the fastest suppression method.

options.label: C
options.text: Treat it like a standard vehicle fire and use a normal hose line attack to knock down the visible flames.

node-2.title: Reignition After Initial Knockdown
node-2.situation: The visible flames appear extinguished after initial water application, but the battery re-ignites 3 minutes later. The thermal runaway is propagating through battery cells.
node-2.question: What is the correct response to reignition?

options.label: A
options.text: Continue sustained high-volume water cooling of the battery pack — EV fires require continuous cooling for 30–60 minutes or more until cell temperatures stabilise. Do not declare the fire out.

options.label: B
options.text: The fire is out — pack up and leave. Reignition is unlikely.

options.label: C
options.text: Cut the high-voltage cables to stop the fire — this will interrupt the thermal runaway.

node-3.title: Crew Safety and PPE
node-3.situation: EV battery smoke contains toxic gases including hydrogen fluoride and carbon monoxide. Crew members not wearing SCBA are reporting irritation near the perimeter.
node-3.question: How do you manage crew safety?

options.label: A
options.text: Enforce SCBA for all personnel within the hazard zone. Expand the perimeter to keep unprotected personnel upwind and at least 25m from the vehicle. Notify nearby residents to stay indoors.

options.label: B
options.text: Only the crew directly applying water needs SCBA — others can stay without it.

node-4.title: Reignition Risk Post-Suppression
node-4.situation: After 45 minutes of sustained water cooling, the fire appears fully extinguished. The driver wants the vehicle back. Battery temperatures are still elevated according to the thermal imaging camera.
node-4.question: What is the correct action regarding vehicle release?

options.label: A
options.text: Do not release the vehicle. EV batteries can reignite 24–72 hours after an apparent extinguishment. Arrange tow to an open, isolated area and advise the receiving lot of the reignition risk.

options.label: B
options.text: Release the vehicle to the driver — the fire is out and they have a right to their property.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The EV battery fire has been suppressed after sustained water cooling. The vehicle has been towed to an isolated holding area with reignition warnings communicated to all parties. Full incident documentation including water usage and battery temperature readings has been completed. Excellent work adapting tactics to EV-specific hazards.
